title: SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via CI
content: [2603.03823] SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via Continuous Integration Computer Science > Software Engineering arXiv:2603.03823 (cs) [Submitted on 4 Mar 2026] Title: SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via Continuous Integration Authors: Jialong Chen , Xander Xu , Hu Wei , Chuan Chen , Bing Zhao View a PDF of the paper titled SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via Continuous Integration, by Jialong Chen and 4 other authors View PDF HTML (experimental) Abstract: Large language model (LLM)-powered agents have demonstrated strong capabilities in automating software engineering tasks such as static bug fixing, as evidenced by benchmarks like SWE-bench. However, in the real world, the development of mature software is typically predicated on complex requirement changes and long-term feature iterations -- a process that static, one-shot repair paradigms fail to capture. To bridge this gap, we propose \textbf{SWE-CI}, the first repository-level benchmark built upon the Continuous Integration loop, aiming to shift the evaluation paradigm for code generation from static, short-term \textit{functional correctness} toward dynamic, long-term \textit{maintainability}. The benchmark comprises 100 tasks, each corresponding on average to an evolution history spanning 233 days and 71 consecutive commits in a real-world code repository. SWE-CI requires agents to systematically resolve these tasks through dozens of rounds of analysis and coding iterations. SWE-CI provides valuable insights into how well agents can sustain code quality throughout long-term evolution. Subjects: Software Engineering (cs.SE) ; Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL) Cite as: arXiv:2603.03823 [cs.SE] (or arXiv:2603.03823v1 [cs.SE] for this version) https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2603.03823 Focus to learn more arXiv-issued DOI via DataCite (pending registration) Submission history From: Jialong Chen [ view email ] [v1] Wed, 4 Mar 2026 08:20:25 UTC (3,311 KB) Full-text links: Access Paper: View a PDF of the paper titled SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via Continuous Integration, by Jialong Chen and 4 other authors View PDF HTML (experimental) TeX Source view license Current browse context: cs.SE < prev | next > new | recent | 2026-03 Change to browse by: cs cs.AI cs.CL References & Citations NASA ADS Google Scholar Semantic Scholar export BibTeX citation Loading... title_zh: SWE-CI：通过持续集成评估代理在代码库维护中的能力
summary_zh: 由Jialong Chen等五位研究者提出的SWE-CI，是首个基于持续集成（Continuous Integration，CI）流程构建的仓库级基准测试，旨在评估大型语言模型（LLM）驱动的智能体在长期维护代码库方面的能力。该研究指出，尽管现有基准（如SWE-bench）已证明AI代理在静态错误修复等任务上表现强劲，但现实世界中成熟软件的开发通常依赖于复杂的需求变更和长期的功能迭代，这是静态、一次性修复范式所无法捕捉的。SWE-CI试图弥合这一差距，将代码生成的评估范式从静态、短期的“功能正确性”转向动态、长期的“可维护性”。，该基准包含100个任务，每个任务平均对应一个真实代码仓库中长达233天、包含71个连续提交的演化历史。SWE-CI要求智能体通过数十轮的分析与编码迭代，系统地解决这些任务，从而模拟在持续集成环境中长期维护代码的完整流程。这一设计使得评估能够深入考察智能体在长期演化过程中维持代码质量、适应需求变化以及进行持续迭代的综合能力。，SWE-CI为研究社区提供了新的工具和视角，以更贴近实际软件开发流程的方式评估AI编程代理的效能。其核心价值在于推动对AI代理在软件工程中长期、动态作用的研究，而不仅限于孤立的即时修复任务。该论文已提交至arXiv，编号为arXiv:2603.03823，属于软件工程（cs.SE）、人工智能（cs.AI）以及计算与语言（cs.CL）领域。

title: LLM Writing Tropes.md
content: tropes.md - Tropes tropes.md A single file containing all cataloged AI writing tropes. Add it to your AI's system prompt to help it avoid these patterns (let's play cat and mouse!). Disclaimer: Creation of this file was AI-assisted. If you thought I was going to write out a .md file for AI myself you must be mad. AI for AI. Human for Human. Copy to clipboard Download # AI Writing Tropes to Avoid Add this file to your AI assistant's system prompt or context to help it avoid common AI writing patterns. Source: tropes.fyi --- ## Word Choice ### "Quietly" and Other Magic Adverbs Overuse of "quietly" and similar adverbs to convey subtle importance or understated power. AI reaches for these adverbs to make mundane descriptions feel significant. Also includes: "deeply", "fundamentally", "remarkably", "arguably". **Avoid patterns like:** - "quietly orchestrating workflows, decisions, and interactions" - "the one that quietly suffocates everything else" - "a quiet intelligence behind it" ### "Delve" and Friends Used to be the most infamous AI tell. "Delve" went from an uncommon English word to appearing in a staggering percentage of AI-generated text. Part of a family of overused AI vocabulary including "certainly", "utilize", "leverage" (as a verb), "robust", "streamline", and "harness". **Avoid patterns like:** - "Let's delve into the details..." - "Delving deeper into this topic..." - "We certainly need to leverage these robust frameworks..." ### "Tapestry" and "Landscape" Overuse of ornate or grandiose nouns where simpler words would do. "Tapestry" is used to describe anything interconnected. "Landscape" is used to describe any field or domain. Other offenders: "paradigm", "synergy", "ecosystem", "framework". **Avoid patterns like:** - "The rich tapestry of human experience..." - "Navigating the complex landscape of modern AI..." - "The ever-evolving landscape of technology..." ### The "Serves As" Dodge Replacing simple "is" or "are" with pompous alternatives like "serves as", "stands as", "marks", or "represents". AI avoids basic copulas because its repetition penalty pushes it toward fancier constructions (I've studied this!). **Avoid patterns like:** - "The building serves as a reminder of the city's heritage." - "Gallery 825 serves as LAAA's exhibition space for contemporary art." - "The station marks a pivotal moment in the evolution of regional transit." --- ## Sentence Structure ### Negative Parallelism The "It's not X -- it's Y" pattern, often with an em dash. The single most commonly identified AI writing tell. Man I f*cking hate it. AI uses this to create false profundity by framing everything as a surprising reframe. One in a piece can be effective; ten in a blog post is a genuine insult to the reader. Before LLMs, people simply did not write like this at scale. Includes the causal variant "not because X, but because Y" where every explanation is framed as a surprise reveal. **Avoid patterns like:** - "It's not bold. It's backwards." - "Fee
summary_zh: LLM写作套路.md是一个旨在帮助AI助手避免常见AI写作模式的资源文件。该文件由AI辅助创建，包含一系列已分类的AI写作套路，建议用户将其添加到AI系统提示或上下文中，以提升文本的自然度和独特性。，文件主要从词汇选择和句子结构两方面归纳了典型问题。在词汇方面，列举了四类过度使用的模式：一是“quietly”等魔法副词，如用“quietly”、“deeply”来赋予平凡描述以重要性；二是“delve”及其同伙，包括“certainly”、“utilize”、“leverage”、“robust”等词汇，这些词在AI生成文本中出现频率异常高；三是“tapestry”和“landscape”等华丽名词，常被用来简单替代“interconnected things”或“field”；四是“serves as”等替代词，AI为避免重复常用其取代简单的“is”或“are”。，在句子结构方面，重点指出了“负面平行结构”（Negative Parallelism），即“It's not X — it's Y”模式（常带破折号）。这是被识别出的最常见AI写作特征，AI用此结构制造虚假的深刻感，将一切呈现为出人意料的重构。文件强调，一篇文章中出现一次可能有效，但频繁使用则是对读者的不尊重。此外，还包括其因果变体“not because X, but because Y”。，该项目的核心价值在于通过预先识别并规避这些模式化表达，帮助生成更接近人类写作风格、更自然多样的文本。它本质上是一场“猫鼠游戏”，反映了人类不断调整策略以优化AI输出的过程。文件可直接用于系统提示，作为约束条件引导LLM（如GPT、Claude等）避开这些套路。

title: New Research Reassesses the Value of Agents.md Files for AI Coding
content: New Research Reassesses the Value of AGENTS.md Files for AI Coding - InfoQ BT InfoQ Software Architects' Newsletter A monthly overview of things you need to know as an architect or aspiring architect. View an example Enter your e-mail address Select your country Select a country I consent to InfoQ.com handling my data as explained in this Privacy Notice . We protect your privacy. Close Live Webinar and Q&A: AI Agent Swarm Patterns (March 26, 2026) Save Your Seat Close InfoQ Homepage News New Research Reassesses the Value of AGENTS.md Files for AI Coding AI, ML & Data Engineering QCon London (March 16-19, 2026): Learn proven architectural practices to scale your systems faster. New Research Reassesses the Value of AGENTS.md Files for AI Coding Mar 06, 2026 4 min read by Bruno Couriol Write for InfoQ Feed your curiosity. Help 550k+ global senior developers each month stay ahead. Get in touch Listen to this article - 0:00 Audio ready to play Your browser does not support the audio element. 0:00 0:00 Normal 1.25x 1.5x Like Reading list Despite widespread industry recommendations, a new ETH Zurich paper concludes that AGENTS.md files may often hinder AI coding agents. The researchers recommend omitting LLM-generated context files entirely and limiting human-written instructions to non-inferable details, such as highly specific tooling or custom build commands. The team behind the work, including Thibaud Gloaguen , Niels Mündler , Mark Müller , Veselin Raychev , and Martin Vechev , justified the research by noting that while 60,000 open-source repositories currently contain context files such as AGENTS.md (and many agent frameworks feature built-in commands to auto-generate them) there has been no rigorous empirical investigation into whether these files actually improve an AI agent’s ability to resolve real-world coding tasks. The researchers (one of whom contributed to the Humanity Last Exam benchmark ) built AGENTbench , a novel dataset of 138 real-world Python tasks sourced from niche repositories. This setup deliberately avoids the bias of popular benchmarks like SWE-bench , which AI models may have partially memorized. The team tested four agents (Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Codex GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.1 mini, and Qwen Code) across three distinct scenarios: using no context file, an LLM-generated file, and a human-written file. The researchers assessed the real-world impact of repository-level instructions by tracking three proxy indicators: task success rates (as determined by repository unit tests), the number of agent steps, and overall inference costs. All chosen niche repositories featured human-written context files; the first two scenarios were tested by removing or replacing those files. The researchers found that LLM-generated context files degrade performance, actually reducing the task success rate by an average of 3% compared to providing no context file at all. They also consistently increased the number of steps the agent took, driving up infere
title_zh: 新研究重新评估AI编程中Agents.md文件的价值
summary_zh: 苏黎世联邦理工学院（ETH Zurich）的一项新研究对AGENTS.md文件在AI编程中的价值进行了重新评估。尽管行业普遍推荐使用此类文件，但研究结论表明，AGENTS.md文件实际上可能经常阻碍AI编码智能体的表现。研究人员建议完全省略由大型语言模型（LLM）生成的上下文文件，并将人工编写的指令限制在无法推断的细节上，例如高度特定的工具或自定义构建命令。，研究团队指出，目前有6万个开源代码库包含AGENTS.md等上下文文件，且许多智能体框架内置了自动生成这些文件的命令，但此前缺乏严格的实证研究来验证这些文件是否真正提升了AI智能体解决实际编码任务的能力。为此，研究人员构建了名为AGENTbench的新数据集，包含来自小众代码库的138个真实世界Python任务，旨在避免如SWE-bench等流行基准测试中可能存在的模型记忆偏差。，研究测试了四种AI智能体（Claude 3.5 Sonnet、Codex GPT-5.2、GPT-5.1 mini和Qwen Code）在三种不同场景下的表现：无上下文文件、使用LLM生成的文件以及使用人工编写的文件。评估通过三个代理指标进行：任务成功率（由代码库单元测试判定）、智能体执行步骤数以及总体推理成本。，关键发现显示，与完全不提供上下文文件相比，LLM生成的上下文文件会降低性能，平均使任务成功率下降约3%，并持续增加智能体所需的步骤数，从而推高推理成本。而人工编写的上下文文件虽然在某些情况下能略微提升成功率（平均约1%），但其带来的边际收益往往被显著增加的步骤和成本所抵消。，基于这些结果，研究团队提出了优化建议：开发者应优先考虑不提供任何上下文文件；若确有必要，则应仅人工编写包含无法被AI推断的、高度具体的项目细节的简短指令，并避免包含LLM可自行从代码中提取的通用信息。这项研究为AI辅助编程工作流的优化提供了实证依据，挑战了当前广泛采用的实践。

title: Verification debt: the hidden cost of AI-generated code
title_zh: 验证债务：AI生成代码的隐性成本
summary_zh: 验证债：AI生成代码的隐性成本，随着AI编程助手（如GitHub Copilot、Amazon CodeWhisperer）的普及，开发者能快速生成代码片段，但这也带来了新的挑战——“验证债”。这一概念指，由于AI生成的代码可能包含错误、安全漏洞或低效实现，开发者必须投入额外时间进行审查、测试和调试，这些后续成本构成了隐藏的技术债务。，核心问题在于，当前的大型语言模型（LLM）如GPT、Claude等，本质上是基于概率生成文本，而非真正理解代码逻辑或上下文。它们可能产生看似合理但实际错误的代码，例如引入过时的API、忽略边界条件或写出存在安全风险的SQL查询。因此，开发者不能完全信任AI的输出，必须像审查人类代码一样严格验证。，技术细节上，验证债体现在多个层面：一是功能正确性，需要编写测试用例确保代码按预期运行；二是安全性，需检查是否存在注入攻击等漏洞；三是性能，AI可能生成低效算法；四是可维护性，生成的代码可能结构混乱或缺乏注释。此外，当AI工具基于有缺陷的公开代码库训练时，还可能复制已有的错误模式。，为管理验证债，业界建议采取组合策略：首先，将AI助手定位为“副驾驶”，开发者需保持主导权并理解生成代码的逻辑；其次，强化代码审查流程，将AI生成代码作为重点检查对象；第三，利用静态分析、安全扫描等自动化工具辅助检测；第四，结合检索增强生成（RAG）等技术，让AI能访问并参考企业内部的可靠代码库以提升准确性。，尽管AI能提升编码速度，但验证债提醒我们，其价值取决于生成代码的质量。忽视验证将导致债务累积，最终可能引发系统故障或安全事件。因此，在享受AI编程效率的同时，团队必须建立相应的验证文化和工具链，以平衡创新速度与代码可靠性。

title: Llm9p: LLM as a Plan 9 file system
content: GitHub - NERVsystems/llm9p: LLM exposed as a 9P filesystem · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert NERVsystems / llm9p Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 1 Star 10 master Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 25 Commits 25 Commits cmd/ llm9p cmd/ llm9p docs docs internal internal .gitignore .gitignore CLAUDE.md CLAUDE.md README.md README.md go.mod go.mod go.sum go.sum View all files Repository files navigation llm9p LLM access via the 9P filesystem protocol. llm9p enables users, scripts, and AI agents to interact with Large Language Models through standard filesystem operations. Write a prompt to a file, read the response from the same file. Supports multiple backends including Anthropic API, Claude Code CLI, and local LLMs (planned). Supported Backends Backend Status Description Anthropic API Available Direct API access with your API key Claude Code CLI Available Uses Claude Max subscription via claude command Local LLMs (Ollama) Planned Run models locally without cloud dependencies The pluggable backend architecture makes it easy to add new LLM providers. What is 9P? 9P is a simple, lightweight network filesystem protocol originally developed for Plan 9 from Bell Labs. It lets you access remote resources as if they were local files. This means you can interact with LLMs using basic file operations ( cat , echo , > , < ) instead of SDKs or HTTP APIs. Why use 9P for LLM access? Universal : Any language or tool that can read/write files can use it Scriptable : Chain LLM calls with standard Unix pipes and shell scripts Composable : Mount multiple 9P services and combine them Simple : No libraries, no dependencies, just files Installation go install github.com/NERVsystems/llm9p/cmd/llm9p@latest Or build from source: git clone https://github.com/NERVsystems/llm9p cd llm9p go build -o llm9p ./cmd/llm9p Usage Start the Server Option A: Using Anthropic API (requires API key) export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-... ./llm9p -addr :5640 Option B: Using Claude Max Subscription (via Claude Code CLI) If you have a Claude Max subscription and the Claude Code CLI installed: ./llm9p -addr :5640 -backend cli This uses your Claude Max subscription instead of API tokens. No API key required. Requirements for CLI backend: Claude Code CLI installed and authenticated ( claude command available) Active Claude Max subscription Mount the Filesystem There are several ways to mount the filesystem depending on your environment. Option 1: Plan 9 from User Space (plan9port) plan9port provides Plan 9 tools for Unix systems. # Install on macOS brew install plan9port # Install on Debian/Ubuntu sudo apt-get install 9b
title_zh: Llm9p：将大型语言模型作为Plan 9文件系统
summary_zh: 项目llm9p将大型语言模型（LLM）通过Plan 9文件系统协议（9P）暴露为文件系统，允许用户、脚本和AI代理通过标准的文件操作与LLM交互。其核心概念是：将提示写入文件，然后从同一文件中读取LLM的响应，从而无需依赖特定的SDK或HTTP API。，该项目目前支持多种后端。Anthropic API后端可直接使用API密钥进行访问；Claude Code CLI后端则允许拥有Claude Max订阅的用户通过本地`claude`命令行工具调用模型，无需消耗API令牌。此外，项目还计划支持通过Ollama运行本地LLM，以实现无云依赖的本地部署。其可插拔的后端架构便于未来集成新的LLM提供商。，9P协议是一个轻量级的网络文件系统协议，最初为贝尔实验室的Plan 9操作系统开发。它允许将远程资源像本地文件一样访问。利用这一特性，任何能够读写文件的编程语言或工具（如使用`cat`、`echo`、`>`、`<`等基本命令）都可以与LLM交互，实现了通用性、可脚本化（可与Unix管道和Shell脚本结合）和可组合性（可挂载多个9P服务）。，安装方式可通过`go install`命令或从源代码构建。启动服务器时，需根据所选后端设置相应参数（如API密钥或指定`-backend cli`）。要使用该文件系统，需要在客户端环境（如使用plan9port工具集）中挂载服务端地址。这使得与LLM的交互变得异常简单，仅需基本的文件操作即可完成复杂的AI调用。

title: AI-Powered F1 Predictions
content: AI-Powered F1 Predictions - Daniel Finch - Software developer based in Cambridge AI-Powered F1 Predictions 06 March 2026 I love the new generative AI stuff. I love Claude, I love GitHub CoPilot CLI , and I also secretly also love OpenClaw. No wait - come back! I love exploring the boundaries of what the technology is capable of. That means throwing it at the hard problems I need to solve at work, or taking on the restructuring of my BTRFS pool after I replaced Windows with Bazzite. In this case, however, I decided to see how it would get on with another thing I love - Formula 1. I’m part of a group of people who participate every year in a predictions tournament. Each race, we are given the names of 4 drivers and have to predict their finishing positions. It’s a bit of fun, there’s no money involved - though there is a trophy for the winner. Generally speaking, my wife and I tend to do reasonably well; last year, we finished 6th of 38, having led the tournament for 5 of the races, and being fairly close to the leaders the rest of the time. This year, we’re taking part again. But I’m also interested to see how the latest frontier models will fare. I’m fortunate to have access to GitHub CoPilot Enterprise through work, so I have access to a lot of the latest models. Additionally, I have access to Google’s Gemini Pro models through their web interface. So, I’ve decided that this Formula 1 season - one that involves a new set of regulations, another team on the grid, and another rookie - will be one in which I exercise the intelligence of AI. I’m not going to submit their predictions in place of my own, as the tournament is very much a human-centric endeavour - one where AI isn’t welcome. Instead, I’m going to take the same subset of drivers given to us in the competition and ask AI where it believes they will place. One additional prediction we make in the competition is “most places gained” - this is a constant throughout the competition, and allows us to choose any driver on the grid as the person that we believe will make up the most places throughout the race. If multiple drivers make up the same number of places gained, they all count as winners. Methodology My plan is to use the latest, most intelligent frontier model from each of the three leading AI companies: Google, Anthropic and OpenAI. For race 1 in Melbourne, Australia, this means Gemini 3.1 Pro, Claude Opus 4.6, and GPT-5.3-Codex. (GPT-5.4 was released after I asked 5.3-Codex for its predictions). For Google Gemini, I will use the “Deep Research” option in the Web UI. For Claude and GPT, I will use the /research command in GitHub CoPilot CLI. If I get access to Gemini Pro via the GHCP CLI, I will switch to it to make them more consistent. Each model will be given the same prompt, in the format of: It’s the 2026 Australia (Melbourne) Formula 1 Grand Prix this weekend. In what positions do you believe Lindblad, Piastri, Perez, Bottas will finish? You can give the same answer for multiple
summary_zh: 软件开发者Daniel Finch热衷于探索生成式AI技术的边界。在2026年F1新赛季伊始，他决定利用前沿AI模型为自己参与的年度F1预测锦标赛提供参考。该比赛要求参与者对每场赛事指定的四位车手预测其最终排名，并额外预测“单场位置提升最多”的车手。，Finch拥有通过工作获取的GitHub CoPilot Enterprise权限，并能访问Google Gemini Pro的网页界面。他计划使用三大领先AI公司的最新前沿模型：Google的Gemini 3.1 Pro、Anthropic的Claude Opus 4.6以及OpenAI的GPT-5.3-Codex。对于Gemini，他将使用其Web UI中的“深度研究”功能；对于Claude和GPT，则通过GitHub CoPilot CLI中的/research命令进行查询。他旨在确保各模型在一致条件下，基于相同提示词生成预测。，需要预测的首场比赛是2026年澳大利亚墨尔本大奖赛，指定的四位车手为Lindblad、Piastri、Perez和Bottas。Finch强调，他不会直接用AI的预测结果替代自己提交，因为该锦标赛是以人为中心的活动，不欢迎AI参与。他的目标是将AI的预测与自己和妻子的预测进行对比，以此评估这些前沿模型在复杂体育赛事预测中的表现。，该项目核心在于测试当前最先进LLM在整合实时数据、历史表现、新规则（2026赛季有全新技术规则、新增一支车队和一位新秀车手）等多变量信息后进行推理预测的能力。Finch希望通过整个赛季的跟踪，深入理解AI在解决此类开放式、不确定性高的现实问题中的潜力与局限。

Here are the latest notebooks and demos using the new model: Intro to Gemini 3.1 Pro This repository contains notebooks, code samples, sample apps, and other resources that demonstrate how to use, develop and manage generative AI workflows using Generative AI on Google Cloud with Vertex AI . Using this repository Description gemini/ Discover Gemini through starter notebooks, use cases, function calling, sample apps, and more. search/ Use this folder if you're interested in using Vertex AI Search , a Google-managed solution to help you rapidly build search engines for websites and across enterprise data. (Formerly known as Enterprise Search on Generative AI App Builder). rag-grounding/ Use this folder for information on Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and Grounding with Vertex AI. This is an index of notebooks and samples across other directories focused on this topic. vision/ Use this folder if you're interested in building your own solutions from scratch using features from Imagen on Vertex AI (Vertex AI Imagen API). These are the features that Imagen on Vertex AI offers: Image generation Image editing Visual captioning Visual question answering audio/ Use this folder if you're interested in building your own solutions from scratch using features from Chirp, a version of Google's Unive
title_zh: GoogleCloudPlatform/generative-ai - 谷歌云生成式AI示例代码与笔记本，基于Vertex AI平台的Gemini模型
summary_zh: Google Cloud Platform 在 GitHub 上开源了 "generative-ai" 资源库，为开发者提供了在 Google Cloud 上使用生成式 AI（特别是 Vertex AI 平台上的 Gemini 模型）的示例代码和 Jupyter notebooks。该资源库旨在帮助用户学习、开发和管理基于 Google Cloud 的生成式 AI 工作流。，核心内容包括多个功能模块的实践示例。其中，"gemini/" 文件夹提供了 Gemini 模型的入门 notebooks、具体用例、函数调用示例和示例应用程序。随着 Gemini 3.1 Pro 模型的发布，资源库已更新了使用此新模型的最新演示和 notebooks。，资源库还涵盖了多个专项领域："search/" 文件夹专注于 Vertex AI Search（原名为 Generative AI App Builder 上的 Enterprise Search），这是一个用于快速为网站和企业数据构建搜索引擎的托管解决方案。"rag-grounding/" 文件夹则集中提供了关于在 Vertex AI 上实现检索增强生成（RAG）和 Grounding 技术的 notebooks 与样本索引。，此外，对于希望从零开始构建定制化解决方案的开发者，该资源库也提供了底层 API 的使用示例。"vision/" 文件夹包含了使用 Vertex AI Imagen API（如图像生成、编辑、视觉描述和视觉问答）的功能示例。"audio/" 文件夹则提供了使用 Google 的 Chirp 模型（通用语音模型版本）构建音频相关解决方案的起步资源。，该项目作为 Google Cloud 生成式 AI 技术的实践入口，通过结构化的代码样本降低了开发门槛，是开发者利用 Vertex AI 平台和 Gemini 等先进模型进行应用探索和原型开发的重要参考。

title_zh: openclaw/openclaw - 您的专属AI助手。全操作系统兼容。全平台支持。龙虾之道。🦞
summary_zh: OpenClaw是一个可在自有设备上部署运行的个人AI助手项目。其核心目标是让用户能在日常使用的各类通信渠道中直接与AI交互，例如WhatsApp、Telegram、Discord、Slack等平台，甚至支持通过短信和电话进行沟通。项目强调“龙虾之道”，寓意是追求坚固、灵活且能适应各种环境的解决方案。，该项目采用模块化架构设计，核心是一个AI网关，负责处理不同通信平台的接入与消息路由。用户可以通过配置文件或UI界面，将OpenClaw连接到多个消息通道和不同的AI服务提供商，例如OpenAI的GPT系列、Anthropic的Claude等大型语言模型（LLM）。其工作流程通常为：从某个通信渠道接收用户查询，将其发送至配置的LLM获取响应，然后再将回答传回原渠道。，技术栈上，OpenClaw主要使用TypeScript开发，并提供Docker容器化部署方案，这为实现“任何操作系统、任何平台”的承诺奠定了基础。项目仓库包含了完整的源代码、详细的文档以及用于持续集成/持续部署（CI/CD）的工作流配置。，OpenClaw的一个关键特点是其注重隐私和用户控制。由于助手运行在用户自己的设备或服务器上，对话数据无需经过第三方云服务，增强了隐私性。同时，项目支持通过技能（Skills）和插件体系扩展功能，允许AI助手执行具体的操作，例如控制智能家居或查询信息。项目文档也提到了对检索增强生成（RAG）等高级模式的支持潜力，以便利用用户自有数据提升回答质量。，总之，OpenClaw是一个开源、可自托管、支持多平台渠道集成的个人AI助手框架，旨在为用户提供一个私密、可控且可高度定制化的AI交互入口。

title: shareAI-lab/learn-claude-code - Bash is all you need - A nano Claude Code–like agent, built from 0 to 1
content: GitHub - shareAI-lab/learn-claude-code: Bash is all you need - A nano Claude Code–like agent, built from 0 to 1 · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert shareAI-lab / learn-claude-code Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 4.4k Star 23.5k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 9 Commits 9 Commits .github/ workflows .github/ workflows agents agents docs docs skills skills web web .env.example .env.example .gitignore .gitignore LICENSE LICENSE README-ja.md README-ja.md README-zh.md README-zh.md README.md README.md requirements.txt requirements.txt View all files Repository files navigation English | 中文 | 日本語 Learn Claude Code -- A nano Claude Code-like agent, built from 0 to 1 Scan with Wechat to fellow us, or fellow on X: shareAI-Lab THE AGENT PATTERN ================= User --> messages[] --> LLM --> response | stop_reason == "tool_use"? / \ yes no | | execute tools return text append results loop back -----------------> messages[] That's the minimal loop. Every AI coding agent needs this loop. Production agents add policy, permissions, and lifecycle layers. 12 progressive sessions, from a simple loop to isolated autonomous execution. Each session adds one mechanism. Each mechanism has one motto. s01 "One loop & Bash is all you need" — one tool + one loop = an agent s02 "Adding a tool means adding one handler" — the loop stays the same; new tools register into the dispatch map s03 "An agent without a plan drifts" — list the steps first, then execute; completion doubles s04 "Break big tasks down; each subtask gets a clean context" — subagents use independent messages[], keeping the main conversation clean s05 "Load knowledge when you need it, not upfront" — inject via tool_result, not the system prompt s06 "Context will fill up; you need a way to make room" — three-layer compression strategy for infinite sessions s07 "Break big goals into small tasks, order them, persist to disk" — a file-based task graph with dependencies, laying the foundation for multi-agent collaboration s08 "Run slow operations in the background; the agent keeps thinking" — daemon threads run commands, inject notifications on completion s09 "When the task is too big for one, delegate to teammates" — persistent teammates + async mailboxes s10 "Teammates need shared communication rules" — one request-response pattern drives all negotiation s11 "Teammates scan the board and claim tasks themselves" — no need for the lead to assign each one s12 "Each works in its own directory, no interference" — tasks manage goals, worktrees manage directories, bound by ID The Core Pattern def agent_loop ( messages ): while True :
title_zh: shareAI-lab/learn-claude-code - Bash 就是你所需的一切 - 从零到一构建的类 Claude Code 纳米级智能体
summary_zh: GitHub项目shareAI-lab/learn-claude-code近日发布，其核心是构建一个名为“Learn Claude Code”的纳米级Claude Code式智能体，从零到一完整实现。项目提出“Bash is all you need”的理念，强调仅需一个基础循环和Bash工具即可创建AI编程智能体。，该项目通过12个渐进式会话（s01至s12）系统性地演示了如何从一个最小化循环演进为一个具备多智能体协作能力的复杂系统。其核心代理模式遵循一个基本循环：用户输入消息数组，LLM处理并返回响应；若响应包含工具调用（tool_use），则执行工具并将结果追加回消息数组，继续循环，否则返回文本。，每个会话引入一个关键机制并对应一句格言。从s01的单一工具循环开始，逐步增加了工具调度、任务规划、子任务分解与上下文隔离、按需知识注入（通过tool_result而非系统提示）、三层上下文压缩策略以实现无限会话、基于文件的任务图（支持依赖关系，为多智能体协作奠基）、后台守护线程执行慢操作、任务委派给持久化队友及异步邮箱、统一的请求-响应通信规则、队友自主认领任务，直至s12实现每个智能体在独立目录中工作以避免干扰。，该项目的核心价值在于提供了一个清晰、模块化的学习路径，深入剖析了AI编码智能体的核心架构与演进逻辑。它展示了如何从最简单的模式出发，通过逐步添加策略、权限和生命周期管理等生产级所需层，构建出功能完备的自主执行系统。所有代码开源，旨在帮助开发者理解并构建自己的AI智能体。

title: virattt/ai-hedge-fund - An AI Hedge Fund Team
content: GitHub - virattt/ai-hedge-fund: An AI Hedge Fund Team · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert virattt / ai-hedge-fund Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 8.1k Star 46.7k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 799 Commits 799 Commits .github/ ISSUE_TEMPLATE .github/ ISSUE_TEMPLATE app app docker docker src src tests tests .dockerignore .dockerignore .env.example .env.example .gitignore .gitignore README.md README.md poetry.lock poetry.lock pyproject.toml pyproject.toml View all files Repository files navigation AI Hedge Fund This is a proof of concept for an AI-powered hedge fund. The goal of this project is to explore the use of AI to make trading decisions. This project is for educational purposes only and is not intended for real trading or investment. This system employs several agents working together: Aswath Damodaran Agent - The Dean of Valuation, focuses on story, numbers, and disciplined valuation Ben Graham Agent - The godfather of value investing, only buys hidden gems with a margin of safety Bill Ackman Agent - An activist investor, takes bold positions and pushes for change Cathie Wood Agent - The queen of growth investing, believes in the power of innovation and disruption Charlie Munger Agent - Warren Buffett's partner, only buys wonderful businesses at fair prices Michael Burry Agent - The Big Short contrarian who hunts for deep value Mohnish Pabrai Agent - The Dhandho investor, who looks for doubles at low risk Peter Lynch Agent - Practical investor who seeks "ten-baggers" in everyday businesses Phil Fisher Agent - Meticulous growth investor who uses deep "scuttlebutt" research Rakesh Jhunjhunwala Agent - The Big Bull of India Stanley Druckenmiller Agent - Macro legend who hunts for asymmetric opportunities with growth potential Warren Buffett Agent - The oracle of Omaha, seeks wonderful companies at a fair price Valuation Agent - Calculates the intrinsic value of a stock and generates trading signals Sentiment Agent - Analyzes market sentiment and generates trading signals Fundamentals Agent - Analyzes fundamental data and generates trading signals Technicals Agent - Analyzes technical indicators and generates trading signals Risk Manager - Calculates risk metrics and sets position limits Portfolio Manager - Makes final trading decisions and generates orders Note: the system does not actually make any trades. Disclaimer This project is for educational and research purposes only . Not intended for real trading or investment No investment advice or guarantees provided Creator assumes no liability for financial losses Consult a financial advisor for investment dec
title_zh: Virattt/ai-hedge-fund - 一支人工智能对冲基金团队
summary_zh: GitHub项目“virattt/ai-hedge-fund”是一个概念验证性质的AI驱动对冲基金系统。该项目旨在探索利用人工智能进行交易决策，但明确声明仅用于教育研究目的，不涉及真实交易或投资，也不提供任何投资建议或保证。，该系统的核心架构是模拟一个由多个具备不同投资风格的AI智能体（Agent）组成的团队协同工作。这些智能体模仿了多位著名投资大师的策略与思维模式，具体包括：，- 估值与基本面分析型：如Aswath Damodaran Agent（专注于故事、数据和严谨估值）、Ben Graham Agent（价值投资之父，寻找有安全边际的隐藏瑰宝）、Phil Fisher Agent（通过深入“小道消息”研究进行严谨的增长投资）。，- 增长与创新投资型：如Cathie Wood Agent（相信创新与颠覆的力量）、Peter Lynch Agent（在实际业务中寻找“十倍股”）。，- 逆向与深度价值型：如Michael Burry Agent（《大空头》原型，寻找深度价值的逆向投资者）。，- 优质企业投资型：如Warren Buffett Agent（以合理价格寻找卓越公司）、Charlie Munger Agent（以公平价格购买优秀企业）。，- 积极主义与宏观策略型：如Bill Ackman Agent（采取大胆头寸并推动变革的积极投资者）、Stanley Druckenmiller Agent（寻找具有增长潜力的不对称机会的宏观传奇）。，- 其他风格：如Mohnish Pabrai Agent（低风险高回报的Dhandho投资者）、Rakesh Jhunjhunwala Agent（印度大牛）。，此外，系统还包含多个功能型专业Agent：，- Valuation Agent：计算股票内在价值并生成交易信号。，- Sentiment Agent：分析市场情绪并生成交易信号。，- Fundamentals Agent：分析基本面数据并生成交易信号。，- Technicals Agent：分析技术指标并生成交易信号。，- Risk Manager：计算风险指标并设置头寸限制。，- Portfolio Manager：做出最终交易决策并生成指令。，需要再次强调的是，该系统仅为模拟，并不执行任何真实交易。其价值在于为学习AI在金融领域的应用、多智能体系统协作以及不同投资策略的建模提供了一个开源的研究框架和代码库。项目使用Python开发，并提供了Docker配置和依赖管理文件（如pyproject.toml）。

title: pbakaus/impeccable - The design language that makes your AI harness better at design.
content: GitHub - pbakaus/impeccable: The design language that makes your AI harness better at design. · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Dismiss alert pbakaus / impeccable Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 58 Star 1.2k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 122 Commits 122 Commits .claude-plugin .claude-plugin .claude/ skills .claude/ skills api api public public scripts scripts server server source/ skills source/ skills tests tests .gitignore .gitignore .vercelignore .vercelignore AGENTS.md AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md CLAUDE.md DEVELOP.md DEVELOP.md LICENSE LICENSE NOTICE.md NOTICE.md README.md README.md biome.json biome.json bun.lock bun.lock package.json package.json vercel.json vercel.json View all files Repository files navigation Impeccable The vocabulary you didn't know you needed. 1 skill, 17 commands, and curated anti-patterns for impeccable frontend design. Quick start: Visit impeccable.style to download ready-to-use bundles. Why Impeccable? Anthropic created frontend-design , a skill that guides Claude toward better UI design. Impeccable builds on that foundation with deeper expertise and more control. Every LLM learned from the same generic templates. Without guidance, you get the same predictable mistakes: Inter font, purple gradients, cards nested in cards, gray text on colored backgrounds. Impeccable fights that bias with: An expanded skill with 7 domain-specific reference files ( view source ) 17 steering commands to audit, review, polish, distill, animate, and more Curated anti-patterns that explicitly tell the AI what NOT to do What's Included The Skill: frontend-design A comprehensive design skill with 7 domain-specific references ( view skill ): Reference Covers typography Type systems, font pairing, modular scales, OpenType color-and-contrast OKLCH, tinted neutrals, dark mode, accessibility spatial-design Spacing systems, grids, visual hierarchy motion-design Easing curves, staggering, reduced motion interaction-design Forms, focus states, loading patterns responsive-design Mobile-first, fluid design, container queries ux-writing Button labels, error messages, empty states 17 Commands Command What it does /teach-impeccable One-time setup: gather design context, save to config /audit Run technical quality checks (a11y, performance, responsive) /critique UX design review: hierarchy, clarity, emotional resonance /normalize Align with design system standards /polish Final pass before shipping /distill Strip to essence /clarify Improve unclear UX copy /optimize Performance improvements /harden Error handling, i18n, edge cases /animate Add purposeful motion /colorize Introduce strateg
title_zh: pbakaus/impeccable - 让您的AI设计工具更擅长设计的设计语言。
summary_zh: GitHub用户pbakaus近日开源了名为“Impeccable”的设计语言项目，旨在提升AI助手（如Claude）在前端设计方面的能力。该项目基于Anthropic公司创建的“frontend-design”技能进行扩展，通过提供更深入的专业知识和更强的控制力，帮助大型语言模型（LLM）克服从通用模板学习导致的常见设计偏差，例如过度使用Inter字体、紫色渐变、卡片嵌套卡片以及在彩色背景上使用灰色文字等问题。，Impeccable的核心构成包括一个增强版的设计技能、17条引导命令以及一系列精心筛选的反模式。其核心技能“frontend-design”包含了7个特定领域的参考文件，分别涵盖排版（字体配对、模块化比例）、色彩与对比度（OKLCH色彩空间、无障碍设计）、空间设计（间距系统、视觉层次）、动效设计（缓动曲线、交错动画）、交互设计（表单、焦点状态）、响应式设计（移动优先、容器查询）以及用户体验文案（按钮标签、错误信息）等方面。，项目提供的17条命令为设计流程提供了具体指导，例如“/audit”用于运行技术质量检查（无障碍访问、性能、响应式），“/critique”进行用户体验设计审查，“/polish”作为交付前的最终润色，“/animate”用于添加有意义的动效。这些命令能帮助开发者审计、评审、优化、提炼和动画化设计。此外，项目还明确列出了需要避免的反模式，直接告知AI不应采取的设计做法。，用户可通过访问impeccable.style下载即用包快速开始使用。该项目致力于为前端设计建立一套精准的“词汇表”，通过结构化知识和针对性指令，使AI生成的设计输出更专业、更独特，并符合现代设计系统标准。

title: 全国政协委员王坚：AI“龙虾”价格很快打下来
content: 全国政协委员王坚：AI“龙虾”价格很快打下来-36氪 账号设置 我的关注 我的收藏 申请的报道 退出登录 登录 搜索 36氪Auto 数字时氪 未来消费 智能涌现 未来城市 启动Power on 36氪出海 36氪研究院 潮生TIDE 36氪企服点评 36氪财经 职场bonus 36碳 后浪研究所 暗涌Waves 硬氪 氪睿研究院 媒体品牌 企业号 企服点评 36Kr研究院 36Kr创新咨询 企业服务 核心服务 城市之窗 政府服务 创投发布 LP源计划 VClub VClub投资机构库 投资机构职位推介 投资人认证 投资人服务 寻求报道 36氪Pro 创投氪堂 企业入驻 创业者服务 创投平台 AI测评网 首页 快讯 资讯 推荐 财经 AI 自助报道 城市 最新 创投 汽车 科技 专精特新 直播 视频 专题 活动 搜索 寻求报道 我要入驻 城市合作 03 月 08 全国政协委员王坚：AI“龙虾”价格很快打下来 2026-03-08 16:28 分享至 打开微信“扫一扫”，打开网页后点击屏幕右上角分享按钮 针对近日火爆AI圈的“养龙虾”现象，全国政协委员、中国工程院院士王坚8日在接受采访时表示，AI“龙虾”会很快便宜下来并普及，任何行业内的人都不会没看到Open Claw的存在。（中新经纬） 下一篇 宏柏新材：股东新余锦宏拟减持不超0.32%股份 36氪获悉，宏柏新材公告，股东新余市锦宏科技合伙企业（有限合伙）拟自2026年3月31日至2026年6月30日，通过集中竞价方式减持公司股份不超过230万股，占公司总股本的比例不超过0.32%；减持股份来源为首次公开发行前取得；减持原因为股东自身资金需求。 4小时前 24小时热榜 查看更多榜单 90 后女首富诞生 OpenClaw掀起龙虾热：行动ASI奇点时刻！全球打工人巨变 全球最小电缸诞生：原法雷奥团队创业、上汽等投资超亿元｜36氪首发 11小时前 林俊旸离职后，再看阿里字节的 AI 路线之争 8小时前 “变压器工厂干冒烟了” 4小时前 林俊旸会去哪？ 11小时前 00后小孩哥10天AI编程，陈天桥24小时砸3000万！靠大四作业当上CEO 3小时前 为什么“从1数到10”这件事，所有视频模型都不会？ 7小时前 关于36氪 城市合作 寻求报道 我要入驻 投资者关系 商务合作 关于我们 联系我们 加入我们 36氪欧洲站 36氪欧洲站 36氪欧洲站 Ai产品日报 网络谣言信息举报入口 热门推荐 热门资讯 热门产品 文章标签 快讯标签 合作伙伴 36氪APP下载 iOS & Android 本站由 阿里云 提供计算与安全服务 违法和不良信息、未成年人保护举报电话：010-89650707 举报邮箱：jubao@36kr.com 网上有害信息举报 © 2011~ 2026 北京多氪信息科技有限公司 | 京ICP备12031756号-6 | 京ICP证150143号 | 京公网安备11010502057322号 意见反馈 36氪APP 让一部分人先看到未来 36氪 鲸准 氪空间 推送和解读前沿、有料的科技创投资讯 一级市场金融信息和系统服务提供商 聚焦全球优秀创业者，项目融资率接近97%，领跑行业
